title: fabric-import Skill template: 3-phase pipeline + .import-state.json checkpoint logic
description: Author the fabric-import Skill template at packages/cli/templates/skills/fabric-import/SKILL.md following the prose-as-program pattern established by fabric-archive (rc.2 8dfa018) and fabric-review (rc.3 49e8917). The skill orchestrates a 3-phase cold-start enrichment pipeline: P1 references the rc.1 init-scan output (does NOT re-implement the scan), P2 mines git log + docs/*.md for proposed pending entries via fab_extract_knowledge, P3 deduplicates against existing canonical via fab_review action:search → reject obvious duplicates, modify-to-merge marginal duplicates. The skill maintains `.fabric/.import-state.json` for resumability after partial runs. Default layer for new entries: team (project artifacts are team-level).

files.change: Author SKILL.md with: (1) frontmatter (name: fabric-import, description, allowed-tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Bash, mcp__fabric__fab_extract_knowledge, mcp__fabric__fab_review); (2) Purpose section: 'Cold-start enrichment of .fabric/knowledge/ from existing project artifacts (git history + docs)'; (3) When to use: triggered by user prompt 'import knowledge' or `fabric import` CLI; SKIP when .fabric/knowledge/ has >50 canonical entries (mature project, use fabric-archive instead); (4) 3-phase pipeline section with P1/P2/P3 narrative; (5) Checkpoint section documenting .fabric/.import-state.json schema and resume logic; (6) Default layer: team rationale; (7) AskUserQuestion contract: only for genuine choices (e.g., per-marginal-dup approve/reject/merge); never for what system can deduce; (8) Worked example: small project import flow end-to-end.

implementation: Read packages/cli/templates/skills/fabric-archive/SKILL.md and packages/cli/templates/skills/fabric-review/SKILL.md fully to lock the prose template structure (frontmatter shape, section ordering, AskUserQuestion contract framing, worked-example style)

Author frontmatter: `name: fabric-import`, `description: Cold-start enrichment skill — mines git log + docs/*.md → proposes pending entries → dedups against canonical. Default layer: team.`, allowed-tools mirroring fabric-archive (Read, Glob, Grep, Bash, mcp__fabric__fab_extract_knowledge, mcp__fabric__fab_review)
Section: Purpose — 1-paragraph statement (cold-start; existing project artifacts; team layer default)

Section: When to use — bulleted triggers ('user says "import knowledge"', 'fabric import CLI command'); SKIP rules ('canonical entry count > 50', 'last invocation < 24h ago via .import-state.json check')

Section: Phase 1 — Init-scan reference (NOT re-implementation): point at .fabric/agents.meta.json + .fabric/knowledge/team/ already-populated state; if missing, run `fabric init` first; what P1 produced (4-7 baseline entries: tech stack, module structure, build config, code style, CI, README first paragraph)

Section: Phase 2 — Git + docs mining: enumerated bash commands (`git log --oneline -n 200`, `find docs/ -name '*.md' -maxdepth 3 -type f`); per-source extraction prompt (call fab_extract_knowledge with source_type='git_log'|'docs', source_id=<commit-sha-or-path>, content=<text-evidence>); idempotency note (fab_extract_knowledge dedups by source_session); checkpoint update after each commit/doc processed (.import-state.json processed_commits[], processed_docs[])

Section: Phase 3 — Dedup vs canonical: for each new pending entry from P2, call fab_review action:search with type+tags filters → if exact match (same title or summary): SKIP (do not propose); if marginal match (semantic overlap >70% by title-similarity): AskUserQuestion 'Modify existing X to incorporate Y?' with 4 choices (modify-existing / keep-both / reject-new / defer); if no match: leave in pending (will be reviewed via fabric-review skill in normal flow); checkpoint update after each pending entry processed

Section: Checkpoint — .fabric/.import-state.json schema (`{phase: 'p1'|'p2'|'p3'|'done', last_completed_step: string, processed_commits: string[], processed_docs: string[], pending_proposals: {pending_path, source_type, source_id}[], started_at, last_checkpoint_at}`); atomic write convention (atomicWriteJson); resume logic: read on skill start; if file exists and phase != 'done', skip P1, resume P2 from last_completed_step or P3 from pending_proposals; document idempotency contract

Section: Default layer rationale — `layer: team` is the default for fabric-import (rationale: project artifacts in git/docs are team-shared; user can later promote to personal via fab_review modify with layer flip)
Section: AskUserQuestion contract — bulleted: 'NEVER asks: which mode, which phase, where to put entry. ALWAYS asks: per-marginal-dup approve/reject/merge/defer (genuine choice).'

Section: Worked example — small project (10 commits, 3 docs) walkthrough: P1 references init-scan baseline 5 entries → P2 mines 8 git commits + 3 docs → 6 new pending proposals → P3 finds 1 exact dup (skip), 1 marginal dup (AskUserQuestion → user merges) → 4 land in pending; final state .fabric/.import-state.json{phase:'done'}

Section: Failure recovery — if P2 mid-process fails: state file preserves processed_commits[], rerun resumes from next unprocessed; if P3 mid-process fails: pending_proposals[] preserved, rerun resumes from where left off
Format SKILL.md cross-checked against fabric-archive/SKILL.md style (markdown headers, code blocks for bash commands, prose-with-bulleted-decisions style)
Verify SKILL.md renders cleanly in any markdown viewer (no broken backticks, no trailing whitespace), token count reasonable (target ~300-500 lines, similar to fabric-review at 368 lines)
Commit as 'feat(skills): fabric-import skill template (rc.4 TASK-004)'

reference.pattern: fabric-archive (rc.2) + fabric-review (rc.3) SKILL.md prose-as-program pattern; allowed-tools convention; AskUserQuestion locked to genuine choices
reference.examples: fabric-archive (rc.2 8dfa018, ~250 LOC); fabric-review (rc.3 49e8917, 368 LOC); both establish frontmatter + purpose + when-to-use + flow + checkpoint + askuserquestion contract + worked example shape

criteria: Frontmatter has name=fabric-import, description (concise), allowed-tools includes mcp__fabric__fab_extract_knowledge AND mcp__fabric__fab_review
Phase 1 section explicitly states 'references init-scan output, does NOT re-implement scan'
Phase 2 section enumerates git log + docs/*.md as evidence sources; documents fab_extract_knowledge call shape
Phase 3 section documents fab_review action:search as dedup mechanism; documents 4-choice AskUserQuestion for marginal dups
Checkpoint section documents .fabric/.import-state.json full schema (phase, last_completed_step, processed_commits, processed_docs, pending_proposals, started_at, last_checkpoint_at)
Resume logic explicitly documented: read state on skill start; phase != 'done' → skip completed work
Default layer = team explicitly stated with rationale
AskUserQuestion contract section explicitly states which questions are inferred vs asked (mirror rc.3 fabric-review structure)
Worked example section provides end-to-end walkthrough with concrete numbers
No grep-detectable contradictions: 'NEVER write knowledge file directly via Edit/Write/Bash' (per fabric-archive convention) appears at least once
Markdown renders cleanly: 0 unclosed backticks, 0 broken links

rationale.chosen_approach: Pure prose template; mirrors fabric-archive/review patterns; .import-state.json checkpoint schema is part of skill prose (not a separate JSON schema file) since the skill is the only consumer
alternatives_considered: Separate fab_import MCP tool — rejected: skill internalizes the prompt + flow; MCP tool would just be a wrapper around extract+search; matches rc.2 boundary decision
Embed P1 init-scan as Phase 0 within the skill — rejected: init-scan already lands in rc.1 init.ts as deterministic CLI step; skill SHOULD reference, not duplicate
JSON schema file for .import-state.json — rejected: only the skill reads/writes it; no cross-tool consumer
Default layer = personal — rejected: handoff explicitly says team (project artifacts are team-level)
decision_factors: Pattern reuse with fabric-archive/review for consistency
Skill is prose-as-program; logic lives in markdown for the AI to interpret
Checkpoint design is internal to skill; no shared schema needed
Default layer must match handoff acceptance criterion
rationale.tradeoffs: Prose-as-program limits machine-checkability of skill logic; mitigated by grep-verifiable section headers in convergence criteria + worked example providing executable trace

manual_checks: Read the authored SKILL.md end-to-end; verify it teaches the 3-phase flow without ambiguity
Verify it states 'default layer: team' explicitly and once
Verify the checkpoint schema is fully spelled out (all fields named)
Diff against fabric-archive + fabric-review SKILL.md — confirm consistent style
success_metrics: Markdown linter (markdownlint or equivalent) passes 0 errors
wc -w of body section is reasonable (1500-3000 words target)

risks.description: Skill prose may drift from fab_extract_knowledge / fab_review actual contracts (e.g., parameter names) — agents executing the skill could fail
risks.mitigation: Cross-check fab_extract_knowledge inputs against packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts FabExtractKnowledgeInput schema while authoring; cross-check fab_review search action against FabReviewSearchInput schema; cite line numbers in code comments within the SKILL.md for traceability
risks.fallback: If contract drift detected during dogfood (TASK-009), update SKILL.md inline + commit as 'fix(skill): align fabric-import with fab_review search contract'

risks.description: Worked example may not actually work end-to-end if a step is missing
risks.mitigation: Author worked example AFTER all flow sections are complete; trace each step against actual MCP tool surface; include concrete pending_path values that match canonical naming

risks.description: Marginal-dup detection threshold (>70% title similarity) is hand-wavy; agent interpretation may vary
risks.mitigation: Frame as guidance not algorithm: 'AskUserQuestion when uncertain'; agent surfaces ambiguity to user rather than auto-merging; document in skill
risks.fallback: Tighten threshold or add fuzzy-match library in v2.1 if user reports false positives

key_functions.purpose: Frontmatter + Purpose + When-to-use + Phase 1 + Phase 2 + Phase 3 + Checkpoint + Default-layer + AskUserQuestion-contract + Worked-example + Failure-recovery
